title: NOC 32100 – Opticians | GTR Immigration
description: Opticians under NOC 32100 are in steady demand across the vision care and healthcare sector in Canada. With growing awareness of eye health, an aging population, and expanded access to optical services, qualified opticians continue to find strong employment opportunities nationwide. As a TEER 2 occupation, Opticians are eligible under multiple economic immigration pathways, making this role a practical and structured pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER2 – College diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group32 – Technical occupations in healthSub-Major Group321 – Technical occupations in health (except practitioners of natural healing)Minor Group3210 – Technical occupations in therapy and assessmentNOC Code32100

duties: Opticians perform some or all of the following duties:
Obtain specifications for eyeglasses or contact lenses from prescriptions
Take measurements including pupil distance, eye curvature, and bridge width
Advise clients on lens materials, frames, coatings, and tints
Adjust and fit contact lenses and instruct clients on proper care
Grind, polish, cut, and edge lenses or coordinate laboratory production
Insert lenses into frames and adjust finished eyeglasses
May supervise student or junior opticians
employment_requirements: Completion of a two- or three-year college program in ophthalmic dispensing or optical sciences
Licensing by a provincial regulatory body is required in all provinces
Licensing scope and protected titles related to contact lens fitting vary by province
Examples of provincial regulatory authorities include:
College of Opticians of British Columbia
Ordre des opticiens d'ordonnances du Québec
Internationally trained opticians must complete credential assessment and provincial licensing prior to practice.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range between $34,700 and $61,600, depending on experience, province, and specialization.
job_outlook: The outlook for NOC 32100 remains stable across Canada. Demand is driven by:
Increasing vision care needs among aging populations
Growth in contact lens and specialty eyewear services
Licensed opticians with strong customer service and technical skills have consistent employment prospects.
labour_market_demand: This stable outlook reflects replacement demand and continued growth in optical services. Provinces with active opportunities include Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Manitoba, Saskatchewan, and Atlantic Canada.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 32100?
Mandatory licensing that ensures professional recognition
Opportunities for supervisory and managerial advancement
Access to public healthcare and settlement benefits
Vision care remains an essential service across Canada’s healthcare framework.
pathways: If you are a qualified optician, NOC 32100 offers a practical immigration pathway to Canada.
Eligibility assessment under Express Entry and PNP
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your structured pathway toward permanent residence in Canada as an Optician under NOC 32100.
